---
title: "Additional figures screening"
author: "Miléna"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
targets::tar_load(compounds_samples_spagg_to_keep)
targets::tar_source(here::here("R/analysis_internship.R"))

library(ggplot2)
library(ggfortify)

data <- compounds_samples_spagg_to_keep |> dplyr::filter(!class %in% c( "Sesquiterpenes", "Oxygenated-sesquiterpenes")) |> 
  dplyr::select(-smiles, -class, -inchikey) |> dplyr::mutate(across(c(where(is.character), -compound), as.numeric))|> 
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -compound,names_to = "sample",values_to = "value") |> 
  tidyr::pivot_wider( names_from = "compound", values_from = "value") |> 
  dplyr::mutate(across(-sample, ~ ifelse(is.na(.), 0, .))) |>  
  dplyr::mutate(gragg = stringr::str_to_upper(stringr::str_sub(sample, 1,4)))


sample_col <- data |> dplyr::select(sample, gragg)
numeric_cols <- data |> dplyr::select(-sample, -gragg)

# Normaliser les colonnes numériques
numeric_cols_normalised <- normaliser_dataframe(numeric_cols)

# Réassembler avec la colonne sample
data_N <- cbind(sample = sample_col, numeric_cols_normalised)

pca = princomp(data_N[,3 :29])
  
biplot(pca)

ggplot2::autoplot(
  pca,
  data = data_N,
  colour = "sample.gragg",      # Colorer par cette variable
  label = TRUE,
  label.label = "sample.gragg",  # Afficher ces labels
  label.size = 3,
  loadings = TRUE,
  loadings.label = TRUE,
  loadings.colour = "lightgray",        # Flèches en gris clair
  loadings.label.colour = "lightgray"   # Labels des flèches en gris clair
) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(colour = "Sample Group")  # Renommer la légende
```

```{r}
#| warning: false

library(mdatools)
targets::tar_load(data_set_PLSDA)
m.all = plsda(data_set_PLSDA[[1]], data_set_PLSDA[[2]], 3, cv = 1)
# Extract scores
scores <- m.all$res$cal$xdecomp$scores
scores_df <- data.frame(
  Comp1 = scores[, 1],
  Comp2 = scores[, 2],
  Class = data_set_PLSDA[[2]]
)

class_counts <- table(scores_df$Class)
valid_classes <- names(class_counts[class_counts >= 3])

# Create a filtered version for ellipses only
scores_df_filtered <- scores_df[scores_df$Class %in% valid_classes, ]

# Create plot with ggplot2
ggplot(scores_df, aes(x = Comp1, y = Comp2, color = Class)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  # Add ellipses only for classes with enough points
  stat_ellipse(data = scores_df_filtered, 
               aes(x = Comp1, y = Comp2, color = Class),
               level = 0.95, type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PLS-DA Score Plot",
       x = paste0("Component 1 (", round(m.all$calres$xdecomp$expvar[1], 1), "%)"),
       y = paste0("Component 2 (", round(m.all$calres$xdecomp$expvar[2], 1), "%)")) +
  theme(legend.position = "right") +
  # Use a color palette that supports many classes
  scale_color_discrete()

```

```{r}
#| warning: false
 targets::tar_load(data_set_PLSDA)
m.all = plsda(data_set_PLSDA[[1]], data_set_PLSDA[[2]], 3, cv = 1)
# Extract scores
scores <- m.all$res$cal$xdecomp$scores
scores_df <- data.frame(
  Comp2 = scores[, 2],
  Comp3 = scores[, 3],
  Class = data_set_PLSDA[[2]]
)

class_counts <- table(scores_df$Class)
valid_classes <- names(class_counts[class_counts >= 3])

# Create a filtered version for ellipses only
scores_df_filtered <- scores_df[scores_df$Class %in% valid_classes, ]

# Create plot with ggplot2
ggplot(scores_df, aes(x = Comp2, y = Comp3, color = Class)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  # Add ellipses only for classes with enough points
  stat_ellipse(data = scores_df_filtered, 
               aes(x = Comp2, y = Comp3, color = Class),
               level = 0.95, type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PLS-DA Score Plot",
       x = paste0("Component 2 (", round(m.all$calres$xdecomp$expvar[2], 1), "%)"),
       y = paste0("Component 3 (", round(m.all$calres$xdecomp$expvar[3], 1), "%)")) +
  theme(legend.position = "right") +
  # Use a color palette that supports many classes
  scale_color_discrete()

```

```{r}
targets::tar_load(compounds_table)
targets::tar_load(times_compound_sp)
targets::tar_load( valid_samples_mono)
targets::tar_load( data_set_PLSDA)

library(fmcsR)
 
  
  
comp_to_keep <- colnames(data_set_PLSDA[[1]])  
compound_data <- compounds_table[compounds_table$compound %in% comp_to_keep, 1:4]
  


  
  sampledata_relative <- as.data.frame(t(apply(data_set_PLSDA[[3]], 1, function(x) x/sum(x))))
  
  ##
rows_all_missing <- apply(sampledata_relative, 1, function(x) all(is.na(x)))

# On garde les lignes qui ne sont pas entièrement manquantes
sampledata_relative <- sampledata_relative[!rows_all_missing, ]
  
  group_df <- data.frame(
    sample = rownames(sampledata_relative),
    species = substr(rownames(sampledata_relative), 1, 4)
  ) |>  dplyr::mutate(spagg = stringr::str_to_upper(species)) #|> dplyr::left_join( pheno)
  
  
  #group <- group_df[,3]
  
  
  #reorder compounds so compound table matches the order of sample data
 desired_order <- colnames(sampledata_relative)

# Réorganiser compound_data selon cet ordre
compound_data <- compound_data[match(desired_order, compound_data$compound), ]

  
  ## debut
  
  
  dis_matrix <- chemodiv::compDis(compound_data, type = c("PubChemFingerprint", "fMCS" ), npcTable = NULL, unknownCompoundsMean = TRUE)
  
```

```{r}
library(chemodiv)


sampDisMat <- sampDis(sampledata_relative, compDisMat = NULL, type = "BrayCurtis", alpha = 1)
divData <- calcDiv(sampledata_relative, compDisMat = dis_matrix[[2]], type = "RaoQ")



chemoDivPlot(
  sampDisMat= sampDisMat[[1]],
  groupData = group_df$spagg
)

```

```{r}

targets::tar_source(here::here("R/plot_screening.R"))
targets::tar_load(pie_chart_emission_screening)
library(dplyr)
library(grid)
library(ggforce)
library(moonBook)
result <- pie_chart_screening(pie_chart_emission_screening) 



p2 <- PieDonut_custom_colors_v2(
    data = result,
    type_colors = c("NE" = "#fce72e", "both" = "#da6484",
                    "iso" = "#70c230", "mono" = "#3764da"),
    subtype_colors = c(
        "both_high-high" = "#c34f70",
        "both_high-medium" = "#eba5b5",
        "both_high-low" = "#f4c2cd",
        "iso_high" = "#59ac24",
        "iso_medium" = "#adda96",
        "mono_high" = "#2c52c4",
        "mono_medium" = "#91a2e9",
        "mono_low" = "#b7c2f3"
    ),
    type_labels = c(
        "NE" = "NE",
        "both" = "Both" ,
        "iso" = "Iso",
        "mono" = "Mono"
    ),
    subtype_order = list(
    "NE"   = c("low", "medium", "high"),
    "iso"  = c("low", "medium", "high"),
    "mono" = c("low", "medium", "high"),
    "both" = c("high-low", "high-medium", "high-high")
  ), use_repel = FALSE, 
    title = "Emission type repartition"
)



plot(p2)
```

```{r}
calculate_all_chemodiv_metrics <- function(sampleData, compDisMat, q = 1) {
  # Check if chemodiv package is available
  if (!requireNamespace("chemodiv", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Package 'chemodiv' is required but not installed.")
  }
  
  # All metric types that calcDiv can compute
  metric_types <- c("Shannon", "Simpson", "HillDiv", "FuncHillDiv",
                    "MPD", "RaoQ", "PielouEven", "HillEven")
  
  # Compute all metrics in one call
  metrics_df <- chemodiv::calcDiv(
    sampleData = sampleData,
    compDisMat = compDisMat,
    type = metric_types,
    q = q
  )
  
  # Compute richness (number of compounds with relative concentration > 0)
  richness <- apply(sampleData, 1, function(row) sum(row > 0))
  
  # Get list of compound names present in each sample
  compound_names <- colnames(sampleData)
  compounds_present <- apply(sampleData, 1, function(row) {
    present <- compound_names[row > 0]
    paste(present, collapse = ", ")
  })
  
  # Add these as new columns
  metrics_df$Richness <- richness
  metrics_df$Compounds <- compounds_present
  
  # Ensure row names are preserved (calcDiv already returns row names from sampleData)
  # But we can explicitly set them
  rownames(metrics_df) <- rownames(sampleData)
  
  return(metrics_df)
}


test <- calculate_all_chemodiv_metrics(sampledata_relative, dis_matrix[[2]], q = 1)


test$rowname <- rownames(test)                
test$prefixe <- toupper(substr(test$rowname, 1, 4))



sum_by_sample <- rowSums(data_set_PLSDA[[3]])


test$sum_by_sample <- sum_by_sample[test$rowname]

```

Est-ce qu'il ne faudrait pasplutot faire ce type de graph sans enlever les singletons ?

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Liste des colonnes de diversité
diversity_metrics <- c("Shannon", "Simpson", "HillDiv", "FuncHillDiv", "MPD", "RaoQ", 
                       "PielouEven", "HillEven", "Richness")
diversity_metrics <- intersect(diversity_metrics, colnames(test))

# Boucle sur chaque métrique
for (metric in diversity_metrics) {
  p <- ggplot(test, aes(x = sum_by_sample, y = .data[[metric]], color = prefixe)) +
    geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
    labs(title = paste(metric, "vs sum_by_sample (coloré par espèce)"),
         x = "Sum by sample",
         y = metric,
         color = "Espèce") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "right",                # légende sur le côté
          legend.key.size = unit(0.3, "cm"),       # taille des symboles réduite
          legend.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0),      # marges internes nulles
          legend.spacing.x = unit(0.1, "cm"),      # espace horizontal entre clé et label
          legend.spacing.y = unit(0.05, "cm"))     # espace vertical entre éléments
  
  print(p)
}
```

\
